displayTitle: Reverse It, Shrink It, Prove It

beats.narration: Switch the current off, and the magnetism stays. That leftover field is residual magnetism — and hard steels hold it tightest.

payload.title: DEMAGNETIZATION
payload.sub: The field that stays — and how to strip it out.

beats.narration: A magnetized part grabs machining chips, grinds debris into bearings, throws a weld arc around — arc blow — and many specs demand demag.

payload.heading: Four reasons to strip the field

beats.narration: Every method works the same way: keep reversing the field while it shrinks to zero. Start at or above the original force, then taper.

payload.heading: Reverse and diminish — down to zero
payload.note: START AT OR ABOVE THE ORIGINAL FIELD · TAPER

beats.narration: The A C coil strips surface fields — walk the part slowly out along the axis. Deep D C fields need reversing, step-down direct current.

payload.heading: Match the method to the depth
payload.note: SKIN EFFECT: AC STAYS SHALLOW · DEEP FIELD → DC

beats.narration: So the rule: reverse it, shrink it, prove it. Check the ends — three gauss is the common limit — then write down what you measured.

payload.sub: Your written procedure sets the number.
